DESCRIPTION: Literal mapping of the Australian Signals Directorate (ASD) Essential Eight Maturity Model (Last updated November 2023, Appendix A: Maturity Level One) to AWS infrastructure checks. Each Requirement is one literal clause from the ASD document, in canonical document order: (1) Patch applications, (2) Patch operating systems, (3) Multi-factor authentication, (4) Restrict administrative privileges, (5) Application control, (6) Restrict Microsoft Office macros, (7) User application hardening, (8) Regular backups. The Essential Eight is designed to protect internet-connected information technology networks (workstations, internet-facing servers, online services). Several controls are inherently endpoint or procedural and have no AWS infrastructure equivalent - those clauses are flagged with CloudApplicability `non-applicable` and AssessmentStatus `Manual`. ML2 and ML3 are out of scope of this framework.
REQUIREMENTS_DESCRIPTION: Privileged user accounts explicitly authorised to access online services are strictly limited to only what is required for users and services to undertake their duties.
REQUIREMENTS_ATTRIBUTES_MITIGATEDTHREATS: Excessive standing privilege, Privilege escalation, Confused deputy
REQUIREMENTS_ATTRIBUTES_DESCRIPTION: Least-privilege enforcement on AWS IAM. This is the cleanest cloud-native mapping in the entire framework: Prowler can verify policy scoping, privilege escalation paths and Access Analyzer findings.
REQUIREMENTS_ATTRIBUTES_RATIONALESTATEME: Strictly-scoped privileged access reduces blast radius of compromise.
REQUIREMENTS_ATTRIBUTES_REMEDIATIONPROCE: Audit and tighten managed/inline policies; remove privilege-escalation paths; attach policies only to groups/roles; enable Access Analyzer and resolve findings.
REQUIREMENTS_ATTRIBUTES_ADDITIONALINFORM: ASD Essential Eight ML1 - Restrict administrative privileges - clause 4.

Several controls are inherently endpoint or procedural and have no AWS infrastructure equivalent - those clauses are flagged with CloudApplicability `non-applicable` and AssessmentStatus `Manual`. ML2 and ML3 are out of scope of this framework.
REQUIREMENTS_DESCRIPTION: Privileged user accounts explicitly authorised to access online services are strictly limited to only what is required for users and services to undertake their duties.
REQUIREMENTS_ATTRIBUTES_MITIGATEDTHREATS: Excessive standing privilege, Privilege escalation, Confused deputy
REQUIREMENTS_ATTRIBUTES_DESCRIPTION: Least-privilege enforcement on AWS IAM. This is the cleanest cloud-native mapping in the entire framework: Prowler can verify policy scoping, privilege escalation paths and Access Analyzer findings.
REQUIREMENTS_ATTRIBUTES_RATIONALESTATEME: Strictly-scoped privileged access reduces blast radius of compromise.
REQUIREMENTS_ATTRIBUTES_REMEDIATIONPROCE: Audit and tighten managed/inline policies; remove privilege-escalation paths; attach policies only to groups/roles; enable Access Analyzer and resolve findings.
REQUIREMENTS_ATTRIBUTES_ADDITIONALINFORM: ASD Essential Eight ML1 - Restrict administrative privileges - clause 4.
STATUSEXTENDED: IAM Access Analyzer in account 162665602291 is not enabled.

The Essential Eight is designed to protect internet-connected information technology networks (workstations, internet-facing servers, online services). REQUIREMENTS_DESCRIPTION: Backups of data, applications and settings are performed and retained in accordance with business criticality and business continuity requirements.
REQUIREMENTS_ATTRIBUTES_DESCRIPTION: Backup coverage across AWS data services. Whether the backup frequency and retention align with business criticality is an operator classification.
REQUIREMENTS_ATTRIBUTES_RATIONALESTATEME: Backups must exist and align with the criticality of the data they protect.
REQUIREMENTS_ATTRIBUTES_IMPACTSTATEMENT: Prowler verifies backup mechanisms exist; criticality alignment is procedural.
REQUIREMENTS_ATTRIBUTES_REMEDIATIONPROCE: Define AWS Backup plans + vaults covering critical services; enable engine-native backups on RDS / ElastiCache / DocumentDB / Neptune / Redshift; enable EFS Backup, EBS Backup, DynamoDB PITR; configure DLM lifecycle.
REQUIREMENTS_ATTRIBUTES_AUDITPROCEDURE: Run all listed checks. Manually verify cadence and retention match criticality.
REQUIREMENTS_ATTRIBUTES_ADDITIONALINFORM: ASD Essential Eight ML1 - Regular backups - clause 1.
STATUSEXTENDED: No Backup Vault exist.
NAME: ASD Essential Eight Maturity Model - Maturity Level One (AWS)

DESCRIPTION: Literal mapping of the Australian Signals Directorate (ASD) Essential Eight Maturity Model (Last updated November 2023, Appendix A: Maturity Level One) to AWS infrastructure checks. ML2 and ML3 are out of scope of this framework.
REQUIREMENTS_DESCRIPTION: Multi-factor authentication is used to authenticate users to their organisation's online services that process, store or communicate their organisation's sensitive data.
REQUIREMENTS_ATTRIBUTES_MITIGATEDTHREATS: Credential stuffing, Password reuse compromise, Unauthorised access to internal services
REQUIREMENTS_ATTRIBUTES_DESCRIPTION: MFA on the organisation's own AWS-hosted online services that handle sensitive data. Prowler verifies MFA on root, IAM users with console access, IAM admins and Directory Service. Whether a given service handles 'sensitive data' is a classification call the operator must make.
REQUIREMENTS_ATTRIBUTES_RATIONALESTATEME: MFA prevents credential-only compromise.
REQUIREMENTS_ATTRIBUTES_IMPACTSTATEMENT: Sensitive-data classification is procedural; Prowler verifies MFA presence on the underlying identity surface.
REQUIREMENTS_ATTRIBUTES_REMEDIATIONPROCE: Enable hardware MFA on root and admin IAM users. Require MFA for all IAM users with console access. Enable MFA on Directory Service. Configure CloudWatch metric filter for non-MFA sign-ins.
REQUIREMENTS_ATTRIBUTES_AUDITPROCEDURE: Run the listed checks. Manually classify which services hold sensitive data and confirm MFA coverage.
REQUIREMENTS_ATTRIBUTES_ADDITIONALINFORM: ASD Essential Eight ML1 - Multi-factor authentication - clause 1.
STATUSEXTENDED: No CloudWatch log groups found with metric filters or alarms associated.
NAME: ASD Essential Eight Maturity Model - Maturity Level One (AWS)

DESCRIPTION: Literal mapping of the Australian Signals Directorate (ASD) Essential Eight Maturity Model (Last updated November 2023, Appendix A: Maturity Level One) to AWS infrastructure checks. REQUIREMENTS_DESCRIPTION: An automated method of asset discovery is used at least fortnightly to support the detection of assets for subsequent vulnerability scanning activities.
REQUIREMENTS_ATTRIBUTES_DESCRIPTION: Asset discovery in AWS is delivered by AWS Config (resource recorder), SSM Inventory and Inspector v2's continuous coverage. The fortnightly cadence itself is not directly observable from AWS APIs - what is observable is whether the discovery mechanisms are enabled.
REQUIREMENTS_ATTRIBUTES_RATIONALESTATEME: Vulnerability scanning is only as good as the asset coverage it has. Continuous AWS-native discovery is the cloud equivalent of fortnightly asset discovery.
REQUIREMENTS_ATTRIBUTES_IMPACTSTATEMENT: Cadence verification is procedural - Prowler verifies the discovery mechanism is enabled, not that it ran in the last fortnight.
REQUIREMENTS_ATTRIBUTES_REMEDIATIONPROCE: Enable AWS Config recorders in every region. Ensure all EC2 instances are managed by SSM. Enable Inspector v2.
REQUIREMENTS_ATTRIBUTES_ADDITIONALINFORM: ASD Essential Eight ML1 - Patch applications - clause 1.
STATUSEXTENDED: AWS Config recorder 162665602291 is disabled.

NAME: ASD Essential Eight Maturity Model - Maturity Level One (AWS)

DESCRIPTION: Literal mapping of the Australian Signals Directorate (ASD) Essential Eight Maturity Model (Last updated November 2023, Appendix A: Maturity Level One) to AWS infrastructure checks. Each Requirement is one literal clause from the ASD document, in canonical document order: (1) Patch applications, (2) Patch operating systems, (3) Multi-factor authentication, (4) Restrict administrative privileges, (5) Application control, (6) Restrict Microsoft Office macros, (7) User application hardening, (8) Regular backups. The Essential Eight is designed to protect internet-connected information technology networks (workstations, internet-facing servers, online services). Several controls are inherently endpoint or procedural and have no AWS infrastructure equivalent - those clauses are flagged with CloudApplicability `non-applicable` and AssessmentStatus `Manual`. ML2 and ML3 are out of scope of this framework.
REQUIREMENTS_DESCRIPTION: An automated method of asset discovery is used at least fortnightly to support the detection of assets for subsequent vulnerability scanning activities.
REQUIREMENTS_ATTRIBUTES_DESCRIPTION: Asset discovery in AWS is delivered by AWS Config (resource recorder), SSM Inventory and Inspector v2's continuous coverage. The fortnightly cadence itself is not directly observable from AWS APIs - what is observable is whether the discovery mechanisms are enabled.
REQUIREMENTS_ATTRIBUTES_RATIONALESTATEME: Vulnerability scanning is only as good as the asset coverage it has. Continuous AWS-native discovery is the cloud equivalent of fortnightly asset discovery.
REQUIREMENTS_ATTRIBUTES_IMPACTSTATEMENT: Cadence verification is procedural - Prowler verifies the discovery mechanism is enabled, not that it ran in the last fortnight.
REQUIREMENTS_ATTRIBUTES_REMEDIATIONPROCE: Enable AWS Config recorders in every region. Ensure all EC2 instances are managed by SSM. Enable Inspector v2.

NAME: ASD Essential Eight Maturity Model - Maturity Level One (AWS)

DESCRIPTION: Literal mapping of the Australian Signals Directorate (ASD) Essential Eight Maturity Model (Last updated November 2023, Appendix A: Maturity Level One) to AWS infrastructure checks. Each Requirement is one literal clause from the ASD document, in canonical document order: (1) Patch applications, (2) Patch operating systems, (3) Multi-factor authentication, (4) Restrict administrative privileges, (5) Application control, (6) Restrict Microsoft Office macros, (7) User application hardening, (8) Regular backups. The Essential Eight is designed to protect internet-connected information technology networks (workstations, internet-facing servers, online services). Several controls are inherently endpoint or procedural and have no AWS infrastructure equivalent - those clauses are flagged with CloudApplicability `non-applicable` and AssessmentStatus `Manual`. ML2 and ML3 are out of scope of this framework.
REQUIREMENTS_DESCRIPTION: Multi-factor authentication is used to authenticate users to their organisation's online services that process, store or communicate their organisation's sensitive data.
REQUIREMENTS_ATTRIBUTES_MITIGATEDTHREATS: Credential stuffing, Password reuse compromise, Unauthorised access to internal services
REQUIREMENTS_ATTRIBUTES_DESCRIPTION: MFA on the organisation's own AWS-hosted online services that handle sensitive data. Prowler verifies MFA on root, IAM users with console access, IAM admins and Directory Service. Whether a given service handles 'sensitive data' is a classification call the operator must make.
REQUIREMENTS_ATTRIBUTES_RATIONALESTATEME: MFA prevents credential-only compromise.
REQUIREMENTS_ATTRIBUTES_IMPACTSTATEMENT: Sensitive-data classification is procedural; Prowler verifies MFA presence on the underlying identity surface.
REQUIREMENTS_ATTRIBUTES_REMEDIATIONPROCE: Enable hardware MFA on root and admin IAM users. Require MFA for all IAM users with console access. Enable MFA on Directory Service. Configure CloudWatch metric filter for non-MFA sign-ins.
REQUIREMENTS_ATTRIBUTES_AUDITPROCEDURE: Run the listed checks. Manually classify which services hold sensitive data and confirm MFA coverage.
REQUIREMENTS_ATTRIBUTES_ADDITIONALINFORM: ASD Essential Eight ML1 - Multi-factor authentication - clause 1.
STATUSEXTENDED: Root account has a virtual MFA instead of a hardware MFA device enabled.
NAME: ASD Essential Eight Maturity Model - Maturity Level One (AWS)

DESCRIPTION: Literal mapping of the Australian Signals Directorate (ASD) Essential Eight Maturity Model (Last updated November 2023, Appendix A: Maturity Level One) to AWS infrastructure checks. Each Requirement is one literal clause from the ASD document, in canonical document order: (1) Patch applications, (2) Patch operating systems, (3) Multi-factor authentication, (4) Restrict administrative privileges, (5) Application control, (6) Restrict Microsoft Office macros, (7) User application hardening, (8) Regular backups. The Essential Eight is designed to protect internet-connected information technology networks (workstations, internet-facing servers, online services). Several controls are inherently endpoint or procedural and have no AWS infrastructure equivalent - those clauses are flagged with CloudApplicability `non-applicable` and AssessmentStatus `Manual`. ML2 and ML3 are out of scope of this framework.
REQUIREMENTS_DESCRIPTION: Multi-factor authentication uses either: something users have and something users know, or something users have that is unlocked by something users know or are.
REQUIREMENTS_ATTRIBUTES_DESCRIPTION: MFA factor type. AWS IAM API does not surface the underlying authenticator type granularly, only whether MFA is virtual or hardware. Hardware MFA on root + admin is the strongest signal Prowler can give.
REQUIREMENTS_ATTRIBUTES_RATIONALESTATEME: MFA must combine factor categories - knowledge alone doesn't qualify.
REQUIREMENTS_ATTRIBUTES_IMPACTSTATEMENT: Prowler cannot universally verify factor type for every identity. Hardware MFA detection is the closest proxy.
REQUIREMENTS_ATTRIBUTES_REMEDIATIONPROCE: Prefer hardware MFA on privileged identities.
REQUIREMENTS_ATTRIBUTES_AUDITPROCEDURE: Run the listed checks. Manually review IdP audit feed for factor distribution.
REQUIREMENTS_ATTRIBUTES_ADDITIONALINFORM: ASD Essential Eight ML1 - Multi-factor authentication - clause 7.
STATUSEXTENDED: Root account has a virtual MFA instead of a hardware MFA device enabled.

REQUIREMENTS_DESCRIPTION: Privileged users are assigned a dedicated privileged user account to be used solely for duties requiring privileged access.
REQUIREMENTS_ATTRIBUTES_MITIGATEDTHREATS: Compromise of dual-purpose accounts, Privilege accumulation

REQUIREMENTS_ATTRIBUTES_IMPACTSTATEMENT: Prowler flags admin-bearing identities; the operator must verify each is dedicated to privileged duties.
REQUIREMENTS_ATTRIBUTES_REMEDIATIONPROCE: Use IAM Identity Center permission sets or dedicated IAM users with admin policies, separated from daily-driver identities.

DESCRIPTION: Literal mapping of the Australian Signals Directorate (ASD) Essential Eight Maturity Model (Last updated November 2023, Appendix A: Maturity Level One) to AWS infrastructure checks. Each Requirement is one literal clause from the ASD document, in canonical document order: (1) Patch applications, (2) Patch operating systems, (3) Multi-factor authentication, (4) Restrict administrative privileges, (5) Application control, (6) Restrict Microsoft Office macros, (7) User application hardening, (8) Regular backups. The Essential Eight is designed to protect internet-connected information technology networks (workstations, internet-facing servers, online services). Several controls are inherently endpoint or procedural and have no AWS infrastructure equivalent - those clauses are flagged with CloudApplicability `non-applicable` and AssessmentStatus `Manual`. ML2 and ML3 are out of scope of this framework.
REQUIREMENTS_DESCRIPTION: Multi-factor authentication uses either: something users have and something users know, or something users have that is unlocked by something users know or are.
REQUIREMENTS_ATTRIBUTES_DESCRIPTION: MFA factor type. AWS IAM API does not surface the underlying authenticator type granularly, only whether MFA is virtual or hardware. Hardware MFA on root + admin is the strongest signal Prowler can give.
REQUIREMENTS_ATTRIBUTES_RATIONALESTATEME: MFA must combine factor categories - knowledge alone doesn't qualify.
REQUIREMENTS_ATTRIBUTES_IMPACTSTATEMENT: Prowler cannot universally verify factor type for every identity. Hardware MFA detection is the closest proxy.
REQUIREMENTS_ATTRIBUTES_REMEDIATIONPROCE: Prefer hardware MFA on privileged identities.
REQUIREMENTS_ATTRIBUTES_AUDITPROCEDURE: Run the listed checks. Manually review IdP audit feed for factor distribution.
REQUIREMENTS_ATTRIBUTES_ADDITIONALINFORM: ASD Essential Eight ML1 - Multi-factor authentication - clause 7.
STATUSEXTENDED: User aegis-admin does not have any type of MFA enabled.
NAME: ASD Essential Eight Maturity Model - Maturity Level One (AWS)

DESCRIPTION: Literal mapping of the Australian Signals Directorate (ASD) Essential Eight Maturity Model (Last updated November 2023, Appendix A: Maturity Level One) to AWS infrastructure checks. Each Requirement is one literal clause from the ASD document, in canonical document order: (1) Patch applications, (2) Patch operating systems, (3) Multi-factor authentication, (4) Restrict administrative privileges, (5) Application control, (6) Restrict Microsoft Office macros, (7) User application hardening, (8) Regular backups. The Essential Eight is designed to protect internet-connected information technology networks (workstations, internet-facing servers, online services). Several controls are inherently endpoint or procedural and have no AWS infrastructure equivalent - those clauses are flagged with CloudApplicability `non-applicable` and AssessmentStatus `Manual`. ML2 and ML3 are out of scope of this framework.
REQUIREMENTS_DESCRIPTION: Multi-factor authentication is used to authenticate users to their organisation's online services that process, store or communicate their organisation's sensitive data.
REQUIREMENTS_ATTRIBUTES_MITIGATEDTHREATS: Credential stuffing, Password reuse compromise, Unauthorised access to internal services
REQUIREMENTS_ATTRIBUTES_DESCRIPTION: MFA on the organisation's own AWS-hosted online services that handle sensitive data. Prowler verifies MFA on root, IAM users with console access, IAM admins and Directory Service. Whether a given service handles 'sensitive data' is a classification call the operator must make.
REQUIREMENTS_ATTRIBUTES_RATIONALESTATEME: MFA prevents credential-only compromise.
REQUIREMENTS_ATTRIBUTES_IMPACTSTATEMENT: Sensitive-data classification is procedural; Prowler verifies MFA presence on the underlying identity surface.
REQUIREMENTS_ATTRIBUTES_REMEDIATIONPROCE: Enable hardware MFA on root and admin IAM users. Require MFA for all IAM users with console access. Enable MFA on Directory Service. Configure CloudWatch metric filter for non-MFA sign-ins.
REQUIREMENTS_ATTRIBUTES_AUDITPROCEDURE: Run the listed checks. Manually classify which services hold sensitive data and confirm MFA coverage.
REQUIREMENTS_ATTRIBUTES_ADDITIONALINFORM: ASD Essential Eight ML1 - Multi-factor authentication - clause 1.
STATUSEXTENDED: User aegis-admin does not have Console Password enabled.
NAME: ASD Essential Eight Maturity Model - Maturity Level One (AWS)

DESCRIPTION: Literal mapping of the Australian Signals Directorate (ASD) Essential Eight Maturity Model (Last updated November 2023, Appendix A: Maturity Level One) to AWS infrastructure checks. Each Requirement is one literal clause from the ASD document, in canonical document order: (1) Patch applications, (2) Patch operating systems, (3) Multi-factor authentication, (4) Restrict administrative privileges, (5) Application control, (6) Restrict Microsoft Office macros, (7) User application hardening, (8) Regular backups. The Essential Eight is designed to protect internet-connected information technology networks (workstations, internet-facing servers, online services). Several controls are inherently endpoint or procedural and have no AWS infrastructure equivalent - those clauses are flagged with CloudApplicability `non-applicable` and AssessmentStatus `Manual`. ML2 and ML3 are out of scope of this framework.
REQUIREMENTS_DESCRIPTION: Privileged users are assigned a dedicated privileged user account to be used solely for duties requiring privileged access.
REQUIREMENTS_ATTRIBUTES_MITIGATEDTHREATS: Compromise of dual-purpose accounts, Privilege accumulation
REQUIREMENTS_ATTRIBUTES_DESCRIPTION: Privileged AWS access should be exercised through dedicated identities (separate IAM users / dedicated permission sets), not through a daily-driver identity. Prowler can detect IAM users carrying admin policies but cannot infer whether they are dedicated.
REQUIREMENTS_ATTRIBUTES_RATIONALESTATEME: Dual-purpose accounts (admin + daily) increase blast radius of credential theft.
REQUIREMENTS_ATTRIBUTES_IMPACTSTATEMENT: Prowler flags admin-bearing identities; the operator must verify each is dedicated to privileged duties.
REQUIREMENTS_ATTRIBUTES_REMEDIATIONPROCE: Use IAM Identity Center permission sets or dedicated IAM users with admin policies, separated from daily-driver identities.
REQUIREMENTS_ATTRIBUTES_AUDITPROCEDURE: Run listed checks; manually verify each admin identity is dedicated.
REQUIREMENTS_ATTRIBUTES_ADDITIONALINFORM: ASD Essential Eight ML1 - Restrict administrative privileges - clause 2.
STATUSEXTENDED: User <root_account> does not have access keys or uses the access keys configured.

NAME: ASD Essential Eight Maturity Model - Maturity Level One (AWS)

DESCRIPTION: Literal mapping of the Australian Signals Directorate (ASD) Essential Eight Maturity Model (Last updated November 2023, Appendix A: Maturity Level One) to AWS infrastructure checks. Each Requirement is one literal clause from the ASD document, in canonical document order: (1) Patch applications, (2) Patch operating systems, (3) Multi-factor authentication, (4) Restrict administrative privileges, (5) Application control, (6) Restrict Microsoft Office macros, (7) User application hardening, (8) Regular backups. The Essential Eight is designed to protect internet-connected information technology networks (workstations, internet-facing servers, online services). Several controls are inherently endpoint or procedural and have no AWS infrastructure equivalent - those clauses are flagged with CloudApplicability `non-applicable` and AssessmentStatus `Manual`. ML2 and ML3 are out of scope of this framework.
REQUIREMENTS_DESCRIPTION: An automated method of asset discovery is used at least fortnightly to support the detection of assets for subsequent vulnerability scanning activities.
REQUIREMENTS_ATTRIBUTES_DESCRIPTION: Asset discovery in AWS is delivered by AWS Config (resource recorder), SSM Inventory and Inspector v2's continuous coverage. The fortnightly cadence itself is not directly observable from AWS APIs - what is observable is whether the discovery mechanisms are enabled.
REQUIREMENTS_ATTRIBUTES_RATIONALESTATEME: Vulnerability scanning is only as good as the asset coverage it has. Continuous AWS-native discovery is the cloud equivalent of fortnightly asset discovery.
REQUIREMENTS_ATTRIBUTES_IMPACTSTATEMENT: Cadence verification is procedural - Prowler verifies the discovery mechanism is enabled, not that it ran in the last fortnight.
REQUIREMENTS_ATTRIBUTES_REMEDIATIONPROCE: Enable AWS Config recorders in every region. Ensure all EC2 instances are managed by SSM. Enable Inspector v2.
REQUIREMENTS_ATTRIBUTES_ADDITIONALINFORM: ASD Essential Eight ML1 - Patch applications - clause 1.
STATUSEXTENDED: Inspector2 is not enabled in this account.

REQUIREMENTS_DESCRIPTION: A vulnerability scanner is used at least daily to identify missing patches or updates for vulnerabilities in operating systems of internet-facing servers and internet-facing network devices.
REQUIREMENTS_ATTRIBUTES_MITIGATEDTHREATS: Late detection of OS vulnerabilities on exposed hosts
REQUIREMENTS_ATTRIBUTES_DESCRIPTION: Internet-facing servers in AWS map to EC2 with public IP / behind public ALB / NLB. Inspector v2 performs continuous OS scanning. Network devices (firewalls, routers) inside customer VPC are typically appliance AMIs - scanned by Inspector when SSM-managed.
REQUIREMENTS_ATTRIBUTES_RATIONALESTATEME: Internet-facing OS surface is the primary entry point for opportunistic attacks.
REQUIREMENTS_ATTRIBUTES_IMPACTSTATEMENT: Daily cadence implicit in continuous scanning; not directly verifiable.
REQUIREMENTS_ATTRIBUTES_REMEDIATIONPROCE: Enable Inspector v2 EC2 scanning. Ensure network appliances are SSM-managed where possible.

NAME: ASD Essential Eight Maturity Model - Maturity Level One (AWS)

DESCRIPTION: Literal mapping of the Australian Signals Directorate (ASD) Essential Eight Maturity Model (Last updated November 2023, Appendix A: Maturity Level One) to AWS infrastructure checks. Each Requirement is one literal clause from the ASD document, in canonical document order: (1) Patch applications, (2) Patch operating systems, (3) Multi-factor authentication, (4) Restrict administrative privileges, (5) Application control, (6) Restrict Microsoft Office macros, (7) User application hardening, (8) Regular backups. The Essential Eight is designed to protect internet-connected information technology networks (workstations, internet-facing servers, online services). Several controls are inherently endpoint or procedural and have no AWS infrastructure equivalent - those clauses are flagged with CloudApplicability `non-applicable` and AssessmentStatus `Manual`. ML2 and ML3 are out of scope of this framework.
REQUIREMENTS_DESCRIPTION: A vulnerability scanner is used at least fortnightly to identify missing patches or updates for vulnerabilities in operating systems of workstations, non-internet-facing servers and non-internet-facing network devices.
REQUIREMENTS_ATTRIBUTES_MITIGATEDTHREATS: Lateral movement via unpatched internal hosts
REQUIREMENTS_ATTRIBUTES_DESCRIPTION: Workstations are out of AWS infrastructure scope. Non-internet-facing EC2 servers and appliance AMIs are scanned by Inspector v2 when SSM-managed.
REQUIREMENTS_ATTRIBUTES_RATIONALESTATEME: Internal hosts still carry lateral-movement risk.
REQUIREMENTS_ATTRIBUTES_IMPACTSTATEMENT: Workstation scanning is endpoint-management territory.
REQUIREMENTS_ATTRIBUTES_REMEDIATIONPROCE: Ensure internal EC2 are SSM-managed and Inspector v2 covers them. For workstations, use endpoint vulnerability tooling.
REQUIREMENTS_ATTRIBUTES_ADDITIONALINFORM: ASD Essential Eight ML1 - Patch operating systems - clause 4.
STATUSEXTENDED: Inspector2 is not enabled in this account.

NAME: ASD Essential Eight Maturity Model - Maturity Level One (AWS)

DESCRIPTION: Literal mapping of the Australian Signals Directorate (ASD) Essential Eight Maturity Model (Last updated November 2023, Appendix A: Maturity Level One) to AWS infrastructure checks. Each Requirement is one literal clause from the ASD document, in canonical document order: (1) Patch applications, (2) Patch operating systems, (3) Multi-factor authentication, (4) Restrict administrative privileges, (5) Application control, (6) Restrict Microsoft Office macros, (7) User application hardening, (8) Regular backups. The Essential Eight is designed to protect internet-connected information technology networks (workstations, internet-facing servers, online services). Several controls are inherently endpoint or procedural and have no AWS infrastructure equivalent - those clauses are flagged with CloudApplicability `non-applicable` and AssessmentStatus `Manual`. ML2 and ML3 are out of scope of this framework.
REQUIREMENTS_DESCRIPTION: A vulnerability scanner is used at least daily to identify missing patches or updates for vulnerabilities in operating systems of internet-facing servers and internet-facing network devices.

REQUIREMENTS_ATTRIBUTES_RATIONALESTATEME: Internet-facing OS surface is the primary entry point for opportunistic attacks.
REQUIREMENTS_ATTRIBUTES_IMPACTSTATEMENT: Daily cadence implicit in continuous scanning; not directly verifiable.
REQUIREMENTS_ATTRIBUTES_REMEDIATIONPROCE: Enable Inspector v2 EC2 scanning. Ensure network appliances are SSM-managed where possible.
REQUIREMENTS_ATTRIBUTES_AUDITPROCEDURE: Verify Inspector v2 enabled with EC2 scan type. Manually verify cadence via finding history.
REQUIREMENTS_ATTRIBUTES_ADDITIONALINFORM: ASD Essential Eight ML1 - Patch operating systems - clause 3.

Each Requirement is one literal clause from the ASD document, in canonical document order: (1) Patch applications, (2) Patch operating systems, (3) Multi-factor authentication, (4) Restrict administrative privileges, (5) Application control, (6) Restrict Microsoft Office macros, (7) User application hardening, (8) Regular backups. The Essential Eight is designed to protect internet-connected information technology networks (workstations, internet-facing servers, online services). Several controls are inherently endpoint or procedural and have no AWS infrastructure equivalent - those clauses are flagged with CloudApplicability `non-applicable` and AssessmentStatus `Manual`. ML2 and ML3 are out of scope of this framework.
REQUIREMENTS_DESCRIPTION: An automated method of asset discovery is used at least fortnightly to support the detection of assets for subsequent vulnerability scanning activities.
REQUIREMENTS_ATTRIBUTES_DESCRIPTION: Same asset-discovery clause as 1.1, applied here to operating systems. AWS Config + SSM Inventory continuously discovers EC2 instances and their OS metadata; Inspector v2 enrols them automatically.
REQUIREMENTS_ATTRIBUTES_RATIONALESTATEME: OS vulnerability scanning depends on asset coverage.
REQUIREMENTS_ATTRIBUTES_REMEDIATIONPROCE: Enable AWS Config in every region; ensure all EC2 are SSM-managed; enable Inspector v2.
REQUIREMENTS_ATTRIBUTES_ADDITIONALINFORM: ASD Essential Eight ML1 - Patch operating systems - clause 1.
STATUSEXTENDED: Inspector2 is not enabled in this account.

NAME: ASD Essential Eight Maturity Model - Maturity Level One (AWS)

DESCRIPTION: Literal mapping of the Australian Signals Directorate (ASD) Essential Eight Maturity Model (Last updated November 2023, Appendix A: Maturity Level One) to AWS infrastructure checks. Each Requirement is one literal clause from the ASD document, in canonical document order: (1) Patch applications, (2) Patch operating systems, (3) Multi-factor authentication, (4) Restrict administrative privileges, (5) Application control, (6) Restrict Microsoft Office macros, (7) User application hardening, (8) Regular backups. The Essential Eight is designed to protect internet-connected information technology networks (workstations, internet-facing servers, online services). Several controls are inherently endpoint or procedural and have no AWS infrastructure equivalent - those clauses are flagged with CloudApplicability `non-applicable` and AssessmentStatus `Manual`. ML2 and ML3 are out of scope of this framework.
REQUIREMENTS_DESCRIPTION: A vulnerability scanner is used at least daily to identify missing patches or updates for vulnerabilities in operating systems of internet-facing servers and internet-facing network devices.
REQUIREMENTS_ATTRIBUTES_MITIGATEDTHREATS: Late detection of OS vulnerabilities on exposed hosts
REQUIREMENTS_ATTRIBUTES_DESCRIPTION: Internet-facing servers in AWS map to EC2 with public IP / behind public ALB / NLB. Inspector v2 performs continuous OS scanning. Network devices (firewalls, routers) inside customer VPC are typically appliance AMIs - scanned by Inspector when SSM-managed.
REQUIREMENTS_ATTRIBUTES_RATIONALESTATEME: Internet-facing OS surface is the primary entry point for opportunistic attacks.
REQUIREMENTS_ATTRIBUTES_IMPACTSTATEMENT: Daily cadence implicit in continuous scanning; not directly verifiable.
REQUIREMENTS_ATTRIBUTES_REMEDIATIONPROCE: Enable Inspector v2 EC2 scanning. Ensure network appliances are SSM-managed where possible.
REQUIREMENTS_ATTRIBUTES_AUDITPROCEDURE: Verify Inspector v2 enabled with EC2 scan type. Manually verify cadence via finding history.
REQUIREMENTS_ATTRIBUTES_ADDITIONALINFORM: ASD Essential Eight ML1 - Patch operating systems - clause 3.
STATUSEXTENDED: Inspector2 is not enabled in this account.

REQUIREMENTS_ATTRIBUTES_DESCRIPTION: Workstations are out of AWS infrastructure scope. Non-internet-facing EC2 servers and appliance AMIs are scanned by Inspector v2 when SSM-managed.

NAME: ASD Essential Eight Maturity Model - Maturity Level One (AWS)

DESCRIPTION: Literal mapping of the Australian Signals Directorate (ASD) Essential Eight Maturity Model (Last updated November 2023, Appendix A: Maturity Level One) to AWS infrastructure checks. Each Requirement is one literal clause from the ASD document, in canonical document order: (1) Patch applications, (2) Patch operating systems, (3) Multi-factor authentication, (4) Restrict administrative privileges, (5) Application control, (6) Restrict Microsoft Office macros, (7) User application hardening, (8) Regular backups. The Essential Eight is designed to protect internet-connected information technology networks (workstations, internet-facing servers, online services). Several controls are inherently endpoint or procedural and have no AWS infrastructure equivalent - those clauses are flagged with CloudApplicability `non-applicable` and AssessmentStatus `Manual`. ML2 and ML3 are out of scope of this framework.
REQUIREMENTS_DESCRIPTION: An automated method of asset discovery is used at least fortnightly to support the detection of assets for subsequent vulnerability scanning activities.
REQUIREMENTS_ATTRIBUTES_DESCRIPTION: Asset discovery in AWS is delivered by AWS Config (resource recorder), SSM Inventory and Inspector v2's continuous coverage. The fortnightly cadence itself is not directly observable from AWS APIs - what is observable is whether the discovery mechanisms are enabled.
REQUIREMENTS_ATTRIBUTES_RATIONALESTATEME: Vulnerability scanning is only as good as the asset coverage it has. Continuous AWS-native discovery is the cloud equivalent of fortnightly asset discovery.
REQUIREMENTS_ATTRIBUTES_IMPACTSTATEMENT: Cadence verification is procedural - Prowler verifies the discovery mechanism is enabled, not that it ran in the last fortnight.
REQUIREMENTS_ATTRIBUTES_REMEDIATIONPROCE: Enable AWS Config recorders in every region. Ensure all EC2 instances are managed by SSM. Enable Inspector v2.
REQUIREMENTS_ATTRIBUTES_ADDITIONALINFORM: ASD Essential Eight ML1 - Patch applications - clause 1.
STATUSEXTENDED: Inspector2 is not enabled in this account.

NAME: ASD Essential Eight Maturity Model - Maturity Level One (AWS)

DESCRIPTION: Literal mapping of the Australian Signals Directorate (ASD) Essential Eight Maturity Model (Last updated November 2023, Appendix A: Maturity Level One) to AWS infrastructure checks. Each Requirement is one literal clause from the ASD document, in canonical document order: (1) Patch applications, (2) Patch operating systems, (3) Multi-factor authentication, (4) Restrict administrative privileges, (5) Application control, (6) Restrict Microsoft Office macros, (7) User application hardening, (8) Regular backups. The Essential Eight is designed to protect internet-connected information technology networks (workstations, internet-facing servers, online services). Several controls are inherently endpoint or procedural and have no AWS infrastructure equivalent - those clauses are flagged with CloudApplicability `non-applicable` and AssessmentStatus `Manual`. ML2 and ML3 are out of scope of this framework.
REQUIREMENTS_DESCRIPTION: Privileged user accounts (excluding those explicitly authorised to access online services) are prevented from accessing the internet, email and web services.
REQUIREMENTS_ATTRIBUTES_MITIGATEDTHREATS: Drive-by compromise of privileged accounts, Phishing-driven privileged compromise
REQUIREMENTS_ATTRIBUTES_DESCRIPTION: Network-level control: privileged accounts must not browse the internet or access email. In AWS this would map to SCPs / VPC egress controls / dedicated jump-host architecture - none directly verifiable by a posture scan.
REQUIREMENTS_ATTRIBUTES_RATIONALESTATEME: If a privileged account never touches the internet, it cannot be phished or drive-by compromised.
REQUIREMENTS_ATTRIBUTES_IMPACTSTATEMENT: Evidence comes from network architecture and SCP review.
REQUIREMENTS_ATTRIBUTES_REMEDIATIONPROCE: Restrict privileged AWS sessions to a hardened bastion / Cloud Workspace with no general internet egress. Apply SCPs that deny privileged roles from launching workloads with unrestricted internet egress.
REQUIREMENTS_ATTRIBUTES_AUDITPROCEDURE: Manual review of SCPs and network architecture.

REQUIREMENTS_DESCRIPTION: Unprivileged user accounts are prevented from modifying and deleting backups.
REQUIREMENTS_ATTRIBUTES_MITIGATEDTHREATS: Backup deletion by compromised user, Ransomware-driven backup destruction
REQUIREMENTS_ATTRIBUTES_DESCRIPTION: Backup immutability against unprivileged accounts. Cloud-native delivery: AWS Backup Vault Lock + IAM/SCP scoping. Prowler does not currently surface Vault Lock state, so this clause is largely operator-evidenced.
REQUIREMENTS_ATTRIBUTES_RATIONALESTATEME: Without write protection, ransomware that compromises any user can erase backups.
REQUIREMENTS_ATTRIBUTES_IMPACTSTATEMENT: Vault Lock is the strongest cloud control here but is not surfaced by Prowler today.
REQUIREMENTS_ATTRIBUTES_REMEDIATIONPROCE: Apply AWS Backup Vault Lock with retention enforcement. Use SCPs to deny `backup:Delete*`, `rds:DeleteDB*Snapshot`, `s3:DeleteObjectVersion`, etc. for non-backup-operator principals.
REQUIREMENTS_ATTRIBUTES_AUDITPROCEDURE: Manual review of Vault Lock + SCP coverage.
